items.title: Richards, Robinson injuries leave U.S. shorthanded
items.summary: Miles Robinson will miss the United States' friendlies against Belgium and Portugal because of a groin injury and Chris Richards is likely to sit out both games with a knee problem.

items.title: Raphinha ruled out for 5 weeks in blow to Barça
items.summary: Barcelona forward Raphinha faces five weeks on the sidelines after suffering a hamstring injury while on international duty, the club have confirmed.

items.title: Casemiro: Man Utd decision 'made and done'
items.summary: Casemiro has dismissed the idea of reversing his decision to leave Manchester United at the end of the season.

items.title: England v Uruguay: men’s international football friendly – live
items.summary: ⚽ Updates from the Wembley game; kick-off 7.45pm GMT ⚽ Live scores | Follow us on Bluesky | And email Scott The teams are out! England in that aforementioned red, Uruguay in their sky blue. Time for the anthems. God Save the King we all know about; the Uruguayan one really gets the juices flowing, though. And while we’re on the subject of Jonathan Wilson, which we kind of were, he tells a wonderful anecdote about that anthem during today’s edition of Football Weekly Extra. Just before the 45-minute mark should get you there; enjoy, enjoy. We’ll be off in a couple of seconds. Uruguay are not heading into this match in the finest of fettle. Jonathan Wilson reports on that 5-1 shellacking by the USA and some Luis Suárez-infused rancour. Continue reading...

items.title: VI Live: Oranje gaat met gelijke stand de kleedkamers opzoeken voor de rust
items.summary: Het Nederlands elftal oefent vrijdag om 20.45 uur tegen Noorwegen. In deze editie van VI Live blijf je op de hoogte van de vriendschappelijke ontmoeting in de Johan Cruijff Arena.

items.title: The USMNT's weakness at World Cups has been a lack of depth. Has that finally changed?
items.summary: World Cup-winning teams need more than their starting 11, but the U.S. men's national team never had enough good bench options. Maybe that has finally changed?

items.title: USMNT players reveal what 'strict' but 'loving' Pochettino is like behind the scenes
items.summary: The USMNT used to be "inmates running the asylum," but Pochettino has transformed the culture.

items.title: Geblesseerde Lukaku ligt overhoop met Napoli
items.summary: De situatie rond Romelu Lukaku is behoorlijk op scherp komen te staan. De spits van Napoli verblijft nog altijd bij de Belgische nationale ploeg om daar aan zijn fitheid te werken, maar dat is tegen het zere been van de Italiaanse club, die Lukaku zo snel mogelijk terug wil hebben en inmiddels een deadline heeft gesteld.

items.title: Johan Cruijff Arena staat in minuut veertien stil bij overlijden Cruijff
items.summary: In de Johan Cruijff Arena werd vrijdagavond stilgestaan bij het overlijden van Johan Cruijff, dinsdag tien jaar geleden. In de veertiende minuut van de oefeninterland tussen Nederland en Noorwegen gingen de toeschouwers staan om te applaudisseren.

items.title: Bondscoach Senegal: 'Prijzen worden op het veld gewonnen'
items.summary: Bondscoach Pape Thiaw van Senegal beschouwt zijn team nog steeds als de Afrikaanse kampioen, de recente uitspraak van de CAF ten spijt. De Afrikaanse federatie kende de titel aan Marokko toe omdat de spelers van Senegal het veld verlieten tijdens de finale.

items.title: EK-hoop vloeit weg bij Zechiël: 'Het is om je te schamen, schandalig'
items.summary: Gjivai Zechiël stond met een vervelend gevoel voor de camera vrijdagavond. De middenvelder van FC Utrecht scoorde twee keer voor Jong Oranje, maar verloor wel het cruciale EK-kwalificatieduel van Noorwegen . Hij vindt dat zijn ploeg zich moet schamen.

items.title: Tiger Woods involved in car crash in Florida
items.summary: Tiger Woods has been involved in a car crash in Jupiter Island, Florida on Friday, confirmed by the Sheriff's Office for Martin County.

items.title: Super League's 30th birthday LIVE! Hull KR running riot versus St Helens
Salah set completely new standards - Klopp
items.summary: Jurgen Klopp says Mohamed Salah is an "all-time great" at Liverpool and that he "would not be surprised if he plays another six or seven years" after the forward announced he will leave the club at the end of the season.

items.title: Jürgen Klopp believes Mohamed Salah will be irreplaceable for Liverpool
items.summary: Klopp tells former club to quickly move on from forward He calls his Anfield spell ‘a beautiful movie with a happy end’ Jürgen Klopp has described Mohamed Salah’s Liverpool career as “a beautiful movie with a happy end” and claimed the forward’s phenomenal output will be impossible to replace. Klopp was in charge when Liverpool signed Salah for an initial £34m from Roma in June 2017 and, despite the occasional row, remains close to the 33-year-old. The pair exchanged messages on Tuesday after Salah announced he would be leaving Liverpool at the end of the season, 12 months before his contract is due to expire. Continue reading...

items.title: Tennis set to kick-off at Real Madrid's ‌iconic Bernabeu stadium for Madrid Open
items.summary: Carlos Alcaraz could realise a ‌long-held dream by stepping onto the turf of Real Madrid's ‌Bernabeu Stadium, after Madrid Open organisers announced plans to install a practice court inside the iconic venue.

items.title: WSL derby weekend is here but glut of games could have diluting effect
items.summary: Organisers take advantage of no Premier League football with Manchester United v City and Arsenal-Spurs the highlights Derby weekend has arrived in the Women’s Super League and WSL2. Not one, not two, but six local rivalries will be reignited as the divisions try to capitalise on the men’s international break. Is it clever to schedule so many of these clashes on the same weekend though, and especially staging three top-flight ones on the same afternoon? Everton host Liverpool, Manchester United welcome Manchester City and Arsenal entertain Tottenham on Saturday, all within the space of six hours. The answer will probably lie in the attendances. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Irreplaceable' Salah could play for seven more years - Klopp
items.summary: Jurgen Klopp says Mohamed Salah could carry on playing until the age of 40 after the forward leaves Liverpool at the end of the season.

items.title: Djokovic withdraws from Monte-Carlo Masters
items.summary: Two-time champion Novak Djokovic ‌has withdrawn from the Monte-Carlo Masters, ‌tournament organisers said on Friday.

items.title: Does Chukwuemeka show the demise of the 90-minute player?
items.summary: Is former Aston Villa and Chelsea midfielder Carney Chukwuemeka, who has made 97 senior appearances across all competitions without playing a full 90 minutes, an exception or becoming the norm?

items.title: The remarkable return of Roy Hodgson aged 78
items.summary: Roy Hodgson's idea of celebrating 50 years in management is not to sit back reflecting on his career, but instead to return to the dugout at Bristol City.

items.title: Everton to offer David Moyes new deal in recognition of success at club
items.summary: Moyes has turned Everton into European contenders Talks over extending second spell due in summer Everton are planning to offer David Moyes a new contract this summer in recognition of his achievement in transforming the club from relegation candidates to challengers for European qualification. Moyes signed a two-and-a-half-year deal when succeeding Sean Dyche last January, with Everton one point above the relegation zone. They are currently eighth, only three points outside the Champions League qualification places. Continue reading...

items.title: What next for Bellamy and Wales after World Cup agony?
items.summary: Wales' failure to qualify for the World Cup raises big issues for the team, head coach Craig Bellamy and the FAW's finances.

items.title: Roy Hodgson makes shock return to management at 78 with Bristol City
items.summary: Hodgson replaces Gerhard Struber for rest of season He last managed second-tier club in 1982 Roy Hodgson has made a sensational return to management at the age of 78 with Bristol City after Gerhard Struber was sacked by the Championship club. The former England manager, who has been out of work since leaving Crystal Palace in February 2024, will take charge of City for the remaining seven games of the season. They are currently 16th in the Championship. Continue reading...

items.title: "Je ne pense pas qu'ils en soient capables" : Steven Defour très critique envers Anderlecht
items.summary: Anderlecht a arraché de justesse sa place dans le top 6. Un soulagement pour les Mauves, qualifiés in extremis pour les Champions' Play-offs.

items.title: Wat gebeurt er met Stankovic? Vanhaezebrouck zegt wat Inter moet doen
items.summary: Eén van de grote ontdekkingen bij Club Brugge was ongetwijfeld Aleksandar Stankovic. De kans dat hij volgend seizoen nog bij blauw-zwart speelt lijkt bijzonder klein.

items.title: LIVE : Penalty raté par Vermant ! C'est toujours 0-0
items.summary: Les Espoirs jouent un match très important ce vendredi en qualifications pour l'Euro U21. Une victoire, et la Belgique ferait un pas vers l'Euro.

items.title: Rudi Garcia prend la parole sur le cas Lukaku et redistribue les cartes
items.summary: Privé de cadres comme Lukaku ou Courtois aux USA, Rudi Garcia reste calme. Le coach belge en profite pour tester ses jeunes.

items.title: 'Kan hij blijven? Wilmots weet wat hij wil doen met Teuma'
items.summary: De toekomst van Teddy Teuma blijft onderwerp van intern overleg, nu zijn contract afloopt en de sportieve staf van Standard een beslissing moet nemen.

items.title: Le RFC Seraing se prépare à perdre deux joueurs très convoités
items.summary: Hemsley Akpa-Chukwu et Emmanuel Da Costa attirent l'attention de plusieurs clubs. Le RFC Seraing devrait perdre ses deux joueurs cet été.
